commitResults.messageTitle: CLI: Add Gradle scaffold and search loop
commitResults.messageBody:
The repository does not have a runnable Java application entrypoint,
a local listing dataset, or a testable search flow.

Add a Gradle wrapper-based Java 21 scaffold with layered storage,
service, logic, and CLI packages.

Include a repeatable interactive CLI, a flag-driven search command,
startup data validation, and tests for repository loading, filtering,
ranking, and CLI behavior.

This makes Week 8 project setup buildable on the wrapper alone and
keeps the codebase ready for a later GUI layer.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Mark Week 8 setup tasks done
commitResults.messageBody:
Week 8 setup work now includes the runnable Gradle CLI,
local listing data, and a checked build-and-run guide.

Update the README and operations guide with wrapper-based run
commands and the interactive CLI flow.

Mark the remaining Week 8 TODO items done so the checklist matches
the repository state.

commitResults.messageTitle: CLI: Address PR review comments
commitResults.messageBody:
Review feedback highlighted a few correctness and usability gaps
in the scaffold.

Validate unknown CLI flags, make the help text cross-platform,
close CSV readers on parser construction failure, and make startup
travel-time validation use cached bulk coverage data.

Also track the Gradle wrapper JAR so wrapper commands work from a
fresh clone, and align a test assertion with standard JUnit style.

commitResults.messageTitle: build: Add CI quality gate
commitResults.messageBody:
The repository has no required GitHub Actions workflow, Checkstyle setup,
or SpotBugs gate even though main is supposed to merge only after code
quality checks pass.

Let's add a single CI workflow that validates the Gradle wrapper and
runs ./gradlew clean check on Java 21, then wire Checkstyle and
SpotBugs into the Gradle check lifecycle.

Tighten the current code where needed so the new static-analysis gate
passes without broad suppressions.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Record required CI merge gate
commitResults.messageBody:
The contribution docs and ADRs still describe pull requests without the
new required status check, which would leave the repository guidance out
of sync with the enforced workflow.

Let's update the README, fork workflow guide, and repository ADRs to say
that the required CI check must pass before main can merge a pull
request.

commitResults.messageTitle: storage: Simplify travel-time cache access
commitResults.messageBody:
Copilot flagged redundant copying in CsvTravelTimeRepository and the
branch still had a local .gitignore update for VS Code settings.

Return the cached immutable travel-time lookup collections directly,
remove the now-unneeded deep-copy helper, and keep SpotBugs satisfied
with narrow method-level suppressions that document the immutability
assumption.

Also ignore the local .vscode workspace settings folder so the branch
stays clean during local development.

commitResults.messageTitle: ci: Split PR and branch quality checks
commitResults.messageBody:
The single CI workflow runs on both push and pull_request, which makes
GitHub show two similarly named checks on PRs.

Let's split the workflow into a dedicated PR merge gate and a separate
branch-push check, then update the docs to use the new required check
name.

This keeps the required gate obvious in the PR UI and keeps the branch
protection docs aligned with the actual repository settings.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Update TODO release version labels
commitResults.messageBody:
The project now starts its release line at v0.1, but the operational
TODO still points at v1.2 through v1.4 JAR releases.

Update the upcoming milestones to v0.2 through v0.4, switch the release
items to artifact wording, and record the completed v0.1 release.

This keeps the planning checklist aligned with the release history
without changing the separate product-roadmap documents.

commitResults.messageTitle: ci: Add cross-OS PR build gates
commitResults.messageBody:
PR validation currently runs a single Ubuntu-only quality gate, which does not verify that the runnable JAR builds across the supported operating systems.

Add Linux, macOS, and Windows shadowJar jobs on Temurin Java 21 x64, keep the existing Ubuntu clean check, and make PR Quality Gate aggregate those required checks. Update the workflow docs so the documented merge gate matches the new CI behavior.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Update TODO progress tracking
commitResults.messageBody:
The current TODO still shows key GUI milestones as incomplete even
though the related destination, rent, commute-time, and air-con work is
already in place.

Mark the completed Week 9 and Week 10 items, note the GUI progress, and
clarify that the remaining Week 11 feature and UX follow-ups are being
tracked in GitHub.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Address PR review feedback
commitResults.messageBody:
Update docs to explicitly call out Java 21 x86_64 and the GUI run task.

Split formatting, input parsing, error translation, and task startup helpers out of MapsAroundYouGuiApp to improve cohesion.

Rename SearchRow boolean accessor to hasAircon and add tests for ApplicationFactory and GuiSearchService.

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge remote-tracking branch 'upstream/main' into GUI
GUI: Fix missing DatasetMetadata import
GUI: Address Copilot review comments
commitResults.messageBody:
Add GUI-side numeric bounds validation for rent and commute in GuiSearchRequestParser.

Refactor loadInitialData to update UI in onSucceeded without Platform.runLater and collapse duplicate populateDetails catch handling.

Add GuiSearchRequestParser tests for valid and invalid boundary inputs.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Fix invalid multi-catch compile error
docs: Clarify runGui behavior on Windows ARM64
Merge upstream/main after GUI PR merge
Release: Prepare v0.2 with GUI as default entry point
commitResults.messageBody:
Add a Launcher class so the shadow JAR can bootstrap JavaFX without
module-path issues. Set application mainClass to the new launcher and
bump version to 0.2.

Tick v0.2 release item in TODO.

commitResults.messageTitle: Build: Fix ARM64 JDK detection and add arch guard
commitResults.messageBody:
gradlew.bat ARM64 workaround fails when run from x64-emulated shells
(e.g. Git Bash) because PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE reports AMD64 instead
of ARM64.

Let's,
* also check PROCESSOR_ARCHITEW6432 which reveals the true architecture
* search Eclipse Adoptium and Java directories in addition to Microsoft
* add a clear error message in Launcher when run on an ARM64 JVM

JavaFX 21 has no windows-aarch_64 support, so an x64 JDK is required.

commitResults.messageTitle: Launcher: Address PR review feedback
commitResults.messageBody:
Use os.startsWith("windows") to avoid darwin false positive, handle
both aarch64 and arm64 arch strings, and use generic JAR filename in
the error message.

Extract isUnsupportedWindowsArm into a package-private method and add
LauncherTest covering all platform combinations.

Fix "Github" branding to "GitHub" in TODO.md.

commitResults.messageTitle: Search: Add walking, sorting, and saved preferences
commitResults.messageBody:
Complete the user-facing search preference surface across the logic,
CLI, GUI, and storage layers.

Add walking caps, selectable sort modes, result limits, and a
walk-dominant toggle while persisting the last successful search
preferences locally.

Update the docs and tests so the exposed search surface, persistence
behavior, and ranking rules stay aligned with the implementation.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Show full control labels
commitResults.messageBody:
Widen the controls pane and give the form explicit label and input
column sizing so longer field titles are fully visible in the search
panel.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Improve results table sizing
commitResults.messageBody:
Rename the results headers to clearer labels and switch the table
to explicit column widths with horizontal scrolling so the later
columns stay readable instead of collapsing when center space is
tight.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Refine default search layout
commitResults.messageBody:
The default GUI layout needs too much horizontal space to show the
results table and details panel together, and the filters rail still
feels wider than necessary.

Stack the selected-listing details beneath the results table, convert
the filters rail to a compact label-above-control form, and align the
score wording to Match across the UI.

This keeps the default window width more natural while using vertical
space more intentionally.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Streamline search controls
commitResults.messageBody:
The search screen still asks users to manage spacing, sorting, and
walking preferences in ways that feel heavier than necessary.

Let's tighten the filter rail spacing, move sorting to the results
headers, and treat walking quality as a single grouped preference in
the GUI.

This makes the default window easier to scan while keeping the search
results and saved preferences behavior consistent.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Polish header and results table
commitResults.messageBody:
The search screen still leaves the status message detached from the
main header, and the results table does not resize or sort as cleanly
as the surrounding layout suggests.

Move app status into the header row, add more breathing room around the
left controls, and make the results table fill its width with centered
cells and working column sorting for A/C as well.

This keeps the screen easier to scan while making resizing and sorting
feel more native.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Fit result columns at default width
commitResults.messageBody:
The results table still clips the Match column on first render because
its minimum column widths are too large for the default window width.

Reduce the table and column minimum widths so the initial layout fits
cleanly without requiring a manual resize.

commitResults.messageTitle: Review: Address Copilot feedback
commitResults.messageBody:
A few review comments point out one real default-value bug and several
test and validation improvements that are worth tightening.

Use a valid default commute cap, surface a user-facing validation error
for a missing sort mode, and make the affected tests assert the intended
behavior more precisely.

commitResults.messageTitle: Review: Align GUI filters and sort behavior
commitResults.messageBody:
The latest review points out that the GUI walking filter and table sort
behavior have drifted from the intended contract and documented API.

Restore max-walk as an independent GUI filter, restrict backend-driven
table sorting to the supported sort columns, and sync the API spec to
the current balanced-score formula.

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge main into clean-transit-metrics
commitResults.messageBody:
The PR branch is behind upstream main before final review and merge.

Let's,
* merge the latest main branch into the PR branch
* keep the review pass based on the current upstream state

This keeps the branch merge-ready without rebasing teammate history.

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge main into feat-search-updates-clean
commitResults.messageBody:
Resolve the remaining upstream conflicts while keeping the max-transfers
feature wired into the current GUI and data-generation flow.

Let's,
* merge the latest upstream GUI and tooling updates into this branch
* preserve the max-transfers search and CSV schema changes
* keep generated transfer data loadable after PT lookup failures

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge feat-search-updates-clean into 18-20-21-23-search-preferences
commitResults.messageBody:
Integrate the max-transfers feature into the richer preferences and
persistence work so the two PRs can merge without regressing either
search contract.

Let's,
* carry max-transfers through the merged UserPreferences model
* wire the transfer cap into CLI, GUI, persistence, and ranking flow
* refresh the docs and tests to match the combined behavior

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Surface listing constraint values
commitResults.messageBody:
Show walk minutes and transfer counts directly in the results table
and split the selected-listing commute summary into separate
fields.

Update the product and design docs to match the GUI output and
include the scope-MVP requirement ID fix in the same branch.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Remove match column from results table
commitResults.messageBody:
Drop the visible match column so the results table has enough room
for the listing, commute, walk, transfer, and aircon columns.

Keep balanced ordering as the default shortlist order and continue
showing the match score in the selected listing details.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Tighten default results table layout
commitResults.messageBody:
Reduce the table minimum widths and remove extra space around the
results table so all visible columns fit at the default window size.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Align SDD with results table layout
commitResults.messageBody:
Clarify that the results table no longer shows the match score and
that the score remains in the selected-listing details panel.

commitResults.messageTitle: Release: Prepare v0.3 from upstream main
commitResults.messageBody:
Current upstream main already includes the richer search controls,
route breakdown, settings, and persistence work intended for the
v0.3 release.

Let's,
* bump the project version to 0.3
* align release-facing docs with the shipped behavior on main
* correct the CLI entrypoint guidance in the docs and help output

This keeps the release prep branch focused on packaging and
communication rather than adding new release-only features.

commitResults.messageTitle: CliPrinter: Generalize JAR usage help
commitResults.messageBody:
The in-app CLI help should not assume the user is running from a repo
checkout with Gradle and a local build directory.

Replace repo-specific commands with a placeholder fat-JAR path so the
printed usage works for downloaded release artifacts as well.

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge upstream/main into gui-show-constraint-values
Merge upstream/main into branch-Feedback
chore: Prepare v0.4 release metadata
commitResults.messageBody:
The repository still advertised v0.3 in the Gradle version and
release-facing docs even though upstream/main now carries the next
release candidate state.

Let's align the shipped version, artifact examples, roadmap notes,
and release summaries with the v0.4 cut from upstream/main.

This keeps the release PR, published artifact, and user-facing docs
consistent for the v0.4 release flow.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Clarify v0.4 release history headings
commitResults.messageBody:
The new v0.4 release notes sections grouped early user stories under
"Implemented Before v0.4", which made the boundary look fuzzy beside
the existing "Included In v0.3" section.

Let's rename those headers to the actual cutoff they describe so the
release history reads cleanly during review.

commitResults.messageTitle: chore: Add PlantUML render tooling
commitResults.messageBody:
- tools/README.md documents pinned PlantUML v1.2025.2, GPL note, optional Graphviz install
- tools/.gitignore keeps plantuml.jar out of the repo
- scripts/render-diagrams.sh and .bat auto-download the jar on first run and render docs/design/diagrams/*.puml to SVG
- docs/design/diagrams/.gitkeep reserves the target directory for Phase 2 diagrams

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Add Developer Guide, wire UML diagrams into SDD and architecture (Phase 3)
commitResults.messageBody:
Closes #29 — new Developer Guide at docs/developer-guide.md with tick-tock
style structure: Prerequisites, Setup/Build/Test, Architecture, Architecture
& Coupling, Class Diagram, Implementation, Testing, Contribution workflow,
Extension guidance, Pitfalls checklist, Appendix. README links to it under
the Documentation section.

Closes #30 — Generate Shortlist activity diagram replaces the legacy Mermaid
flowchart in sdd.md Workflow B. Commute Breakdown sequence diagram replaces
the Mermaid flowchart in Workflow C. architecture.md ASCII block replaced
with the PlantUML component-overview SVG.

Closes #46 — Dedicated Architecture & Coupling section in the Developer
Guide documents the UI -> app -> logic -> service + storage dependency
direction, ApplicationFactory as the sole composition root, interface-based
constructor injection, the PersistentSearchLogic decorator, and the rules
contributors must follow to keep coupling low as the codebase grows.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Address review findings on DG, SDD, architecture, README
commitResults.messageBody:
- architecture.md: add Application layer section (ApplicationFactory +
  GuiSearchService); split GUI and CLI flows in Data Flow section
- developer-guide.md: narrow 'interface boundaries everywhere' claim to
  what the source actually does (repositories are interface-backed;
  service helpers are concrete but constructor-injected); specify bash
  for fenced code blocks; describe PR Quality Gate as CI aggregate with
  'gradlew clean check' as the local pre-push gate
- sdd.md: align workflow prose with actual call paths (GUI through
  GuiSearchService, CLI direct to SearchLogic); clarify that storage is
  abstraction-backed while service helpers are concrete
- README.md: refresh Current Scope layer list to include gui/cli/app/
  model and ApplicationFactory; drop stale 'GUI work can plug in later'

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Move diagrams to assets/images, fix activity diagram notation
commitResults.messageBody:
Move all PlantUML sources and rendered SVGs from docs/design/diagrams/
to docs/assets/images/ to consolidate all image assets in one location.
Update render scripts, and all image references in sdd.md, architecture.md,
and developer-guide.md to use the new paths.

Activity diagram: remove swimlanes entirely (course reference sheet shows
traditional UML activity without swimlanes). Add skinparam conditionStyle
diamond so decision nodes render as proper diamond shapes matching the
SE-EDU / CS2103DE notation reference rather than hexagons.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Address Copilot review on PR #55
commitResults.messageBody:
- render-diagrams.sh/.bat: add -f to curl so HTTP errors fail fast
- developer-guide.md: remove ambiguous '4-layer' count; say 'layered'
- architecture.md: fix SearchResultViewModel -> SearchRow / SearchResult
- class-diagram.puml: add missing getSupportedDestinations/getDatasetMetadata
  to DefaultSearchLogic and PersistentSearchLogic; add getCommuteDetails
  to PersistentSearchLogic; add sortMode/transportMode fields to
  SearchRequest and UserPreferences; remove duplicate association arrows

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge remote-tracking branch 'upstream/main' into docs/30-uml-developer-guide
docs: Move and render per-layer class diagrams; add §5.1 to DG
commitResults.messageBody:
Relocates GUI/Logic/Storage .puml files from merged PR #54 into
docs/assets/images/ (consistent with PR #55 convention), updates
@startuml names to match filenames, renders SVGs, and adds them
as §5.1 in the Developer Guide.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Clarify no-limit behavior for max transfers
commitResults.messageBody:
PR #53 changed the GUI so a blank Max transfers field means
"No limit", but the User Guide still described blank numeric
fields as invalid and kept steering users toward high transfer
caps.

Clarify the GUI-specific blank-field behavior in the common task
flow, GUI conventions, troubleshooting, tips, and glossary so
the guide matches the shipped behavior.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Add cross-platform runCli examples
docs: Prepare v0.5 release
commitResults.messageBody:
Bump the project version and align the release-facing docs with the
v0.5 cut from upstream/main.

Let's,
* update the runnable JAR examples from 0.4 to 0.5
* add the public demo video link to the README and User Guide
* refresh roadmap, scope, and release-summary docs so v0.5 is the
  current shipped release

This keeps the published artifact, public-facing docs, and release
metadata consistent for the upstream release workflow.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Revert UML path move from PR #59
commitResults.messageBody:
The GUI screenshot additions are useful, but the UML path move adds
extra scope and churn without improving the user-facing guide.

Let's,
* keep the new User Guide screenshots
* restore docs and render scripts to the existing assets/images layout
* drop the temporary diagram_images relocation

This keeps the PR focused and matches the settled upstream diagram
location.

commitResults.messageTitle: test: Improve coverage for service and storage classes
commitResults.messageBody:
Issue #44 calls out direct test gaps in the service, storage, and GUI
helper layers even though broader logic and CLI flows are already
covered.

Add dedicated tests for the missing service and storage classes,
strengthen the existing travel-time and GUI formatter suites, and add
classpath fixtures for dataset metadata parsing.

commitResults.messageTitle: release: Prepare v0.5.1
commitResults.messageBody:
The repo has moved past the published v0.5 release with documentation
refreshes and test-coverage hardening, but the version source and
release-facing examples still pointed at 0.5.

Bump the canonical Gradle version to 0.5.1 and realign the runnable
JAR references plus current-release notes so the release PR matches the
artifact we verified locally.

commitResults.messageTitle: build: Enforce required CI checks for main (#11)
data: Expand demo listing inventory
commitResults.messageBody:
The current app now uses a larger, reproducible app-facing listing dataset.

Expand listings.csv to 180 rows across the covered origin set, document the
generation workflow, and keep the schema aligned with the CLI scaffold.

commitResults.messageTitle: UX: Add persona presets and dark-mode settings
commitResults.messageBody:
Onboarding currently lacks a deterministic way to apply persona-based defaults, and persona/dark-mode preferences aren’t persisted between runs.

We update the onboarding flow to start as “New User” (Student vs Worker), apply deterministic persona preset defaults for the existing rent/commute/ aircon inputs (editable after apply), and persist both the selected persona preset and a Dark Mode toggle via a new Settings window. We also add unit tests for determinism/validation and update the PRD/ user-stories with the exact default values and the “editable after apply” requirement.

Let’s,

Start onboarding as “New User” and prompt for Student vs Worker
Apply deterministic persona preset defaults to the GUI inputs
Keep defaults editable after applying the preset
Persist the selected persona preset and Dark Mode toggle
Add unit tests covering determinism and validation behavior
Update PRD/user-stories with exact default values and edit behavior

commitResults.messageTitle: UI: Created MapsAroundYou logo as window icon
CliApplication: Handle dataset metadata failures in run()
commitResults.messageBody:
CLI banner metadata loading can throw MapsAroundYouException/DataLoadException
before argument parsing, which bypasses CLI error handling.

Move banner printing inside the try block so dataset failures return a clean
exit code and user-facing error message.

commitResults.messageTitle: CsvSupport: Close parser on header validation failure
commitResults.messageBody:
openParser leaks the CSV parser/reader when header validation throws after the parser is created.

Close the parser before rethrowing runtime exceptions to ensure resources are released on validation failure.

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into branch-Feedback
Application: Add GUI search facade and exception base type
commitResults.messageBody:
The GUI now depends on a narrow GuiSearchService API that wraps SearchLogic, and common failures are surfaced via MapsAroundYouException.

This reduces controller coupling to stateful logic ordering, improves error shaping/logging in CLI/GUI, and supports stricter Checkstyle Javadoc rules.

commitResults.messageTitle: MapsAroundYouGuiApp: Restore settings and window icon
commitResults.messageBody:
The JavaFX GUI no longer wires the persona preset prompt, dark-mode
stylesheet, or window icon after recent GUI/service refactors.

Let's,
* use ApplicationFactory.createGuiSearchService() with app-layer DTOs
* re-add the Settings UI backed by UiSettingsStore
* apply dark-theme.css to main/settings windows when enabled
* set the Stage icon from MapsAroundYou_Logo.png
This keeps the UX features working while staying aligned with the
app-layer service boundary.

commitResults.messageTitle: UI: Show match score as percentage
commitResults.messageBody:
The GUI match column displays a 0–1 score, which is less intuitive to interpret at a glance.

Format match scores as percentages in both the results table and the selected listing details panel.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Add MapsAroundYou user guide
commitResults.messageBody: Add a user-facing guide covering GUI and CLI workflows, setup steps, data provenance, local preferences, and troubleshooting.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Fix PR checks for Javadoc and doc links
commitResults.messageBody:
Checkstyle requires public types to have class-level Javadoc, and the user guide referenced a screenshot path that does not exist in the repo.

Add missing Javadoc to MapsAroundYouGuiApp and update the user guide image link to point to an existing asset.

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Preserve persona preset on first-run load
commitResults.messageBody:
First-run Student/Worker choices populate the form before the background task loads UserPreferences from the backend. Applying those preferences afterward replaced preset fields with AppConfig defaults (for example maxTransfers = Integer.MAX_VALUE).

When onboarding just selected a persona, apply only the non-preset preference fields from the loaded snapshot so rent, commute, and aircon stay aligned with the preset.

Document the mapsaroundyou.app layer and dependency direction in docs/design/sdd.md.

commitResults.messageTitle: UI: Add reset-to-defaults for persona onboarding
commitResults.messageBody:
Settings now provides a "Reset to defaults" action that clears the stored persona preset and dark mode preference.

This makes it easy to test first-run onboarding again.

commitResults.messageTitle: MapsAroundYouGuiApp: Wrap reset confirmation message
commitResults.messageBody:
Checkstyle fails due to a line-length violation in the reset settings confirmation dialog message

Split the message into multiple concatenated lines to satisfy the 120 character limit without changing the displayed text

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Make User Guide standalone and add README link
commitResults.messageBody:
End-user workflows and CLI usage details were not clearly centralized and
easy to discover.

Add a dedicated CLI command reference, an end-to-end scenario, and user-language troubleshooting to make expected usage testable.

Link the canonical User Guide from README to reduce onboarding friction.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Improve user guide for first-time GUI users
commitResults.messageBody:
Add a realistic GUI end-to-end scenario and make Match, walk-dominant, and persona preset explanations more practical and accurate.

Move the developer-facing maintenance note out of the user guide into docs/development/documentation-maintenance.md.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Add task flows, error explanations, and glossary
commitResults.messageBody:
Add beginner-friendly common task walkthroughs (cheapest acceptable, shortest commute, and broadening too-strict filters).

Explain common GUI/CLI error states in plain language and add a short glossary for key terms (destination, match, walk-dominant, result limit).

commitResults.messageTitle: UI: Center main layout on wide windows
commitResults.messageBody:
The JavaFX GUI expands to full width on large windows, which makes the controls panel feel pinned to the left and leaves awkward empty space.

Center the main content and cap its max width so extra window space becomes balanced margins while keeping the existing layout and behavior.

commitResults.messageTitle: UI: Show "No limit" for max transfers
commitResults.messageBody:
Max transfers defaults to Integer.MAX_VALUE, which shows up in the GUI as 2147483647 and looks like a bug.

Treat a blank max transfers field as unlimited and display the unlimited saved/default value as an empty field with a "No limit" hint.

commitResults.messageTitle: build: Add runCli task and fix User Guide run commands
commitResults.messageBody:
Gradle's `run` task starts the JavaFX GUI via `mapsaroundyou.gui.Launcher`, but the User Guide documented it as the CLI entry flow.

Add a dedicated `runCli` task for `mapsaroundyou.cli.MapsAroundYouApp` and document CLI invocation using `runCli` (with args passed via -PcliArgs) while documenting `run` as the canonical GUI command.

commitResults.messageTitle: Week 8: Add Gradle CLI scaffold (#9)
commitResults.messageBody:
## Summary
- add a Gradle wrapper-based Java 21 scaffold with layered
storage/service/logic/cli packages
- add a repeatable CLI flow with both interactive and flag-driven search
modes
- add local listing metadata, startup validation, tests, and Week 8
build/run documentation

## Data notes
- `listings.csv` is now the app-facing listing dataset used for
filtering and display
- `Rental_List.csv` remains the shared origin-node dataset keyed by
`Flat_ID`
- `transit_matrix.csv` is the offline commute lookup keyed by `flat_id +
destination_id`
- adding listings on an existing origin id does not require matrix
regeneration; adding a new origin node does

## Week 8 checklist
- mark project scaffold done
- mark local data files done
- mark `build-and-run.md` done

## Verification
- `./gradlew test`
- `./gradlew run`
- `./gradlew run --args="search --destination D01 --max-rent 1800
--max-commute 35 --require-aircon"`

## Review note
This branch is based on fork `main`, so it also includes existing fork
commit `b555e3e` (`docs: Add tP reference PDFs`) because `upstream/main`
does not have it yet.

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge branch 'main' into GUI
GUI: Fix persona selector state for custom presets
commitResults.messageBody:
The settings persona control treated every non-worker preset as
student, so NEW_USER and CUSTOM looked like Student was selected.
Return null when the active preset is not Student or Worker so the
UI shows no selection instead of the wrong default.

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge branch 'main' into branch-UX-first_time_users
UX: Add persona presets, settings menu, and dark mode (#35)
commitResults.messageBody:
Onboarding currently lacks a deterministic way to apply persona-based
defaults, and persona/dark-mode preferences are not persisted between
runs.

We update the onboarding flow to start as "New User" (Student vs
Worker), apply deterministic persona preset defaults for the existing
rent/commute/aircon inputs, keep those values editable after applying
the preset, and persist both the selected persona preset and a Dark Mode
toggle via a new Settings window. We also add unit tests for
determinism/validation and update the PRD/user stories with the exact
default values and the "editable after apply" requirement.

Let's:
- Start onboarding as "New User" and prompt for Student vs Worker
- Apply deterministic persona preset defaults to the GUI inputs
- Keep defaults editable after applying the preset
- Persist the selected persona preset and Dark Mode toggle
- Add unit tests covering determinism and validation behavior
- Update PRD/user stories with exact default values and edit behavior

Closes #22

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge branch 'main' into branch-User_Guide
User guide: Add task flows, troubleshooting, and minor GUI UX polish (#53)
commitResults.messageBody:
## PR title

User guide: Add task flows, troubleshooting, and minor GUI UX polish

## PR description

## Summary
This PR improves the end-user documentation and aligns the GUI with the
guide’s expectations.

Let’s,
- Expand `docs/UserGuide.md` with common task flows, a GUI end-to-end
scenario, clearer explanations (e.g., Match meaning, walk-dominant
threshold), troubleshooting for common errors, and a glossary
- Add a maintainer note
(`docs/development/documentation-maintenance.md`) to keep the User Guide
in sync with CLI/GUI surfaces
- Polish the GUI UX:
  - Keep prompt/placeholder text readable in dark mode
- Treat blank “Max transfers” as **No limit** (uses default), update
parser + add test coverage
  - Center the app content on wide windows for better layout

## Test plan

- [ ] Run GUI: `.\gradlew runGui`
  - [ ] Verify dark mode prompt text is readable
- [ ] Leave “Max transfers” blank and confirm search runs as “No limit”
  - [ ] Resize window wide and confirm content stays centered
- [ ] Run tests: `.\gradlew test`

keep the Ubuntu quality check, and make PR Quality Gate aggregate the
required PR checks.

behavior.

commitResults.messageTitle: Search: add richer preferences and local persistence (#42)
commitResults.messageBody:
## Summary

This PR completes the shared search-preferences surface across the
logic, CLI, GUI, and storage layers.

It adds:
- maximum walking-time filtering
- a user-facing walk-dominant route toggle
- selectable result count and sort mode
- local persistence of the last successful search preferences

## Implementation Notes

- `UserPreferences` is now the single shared preference model across the
app.
- Search ranking supports `COMMUTE`, `RENT`, and `BALANCED` modes with
deterministic tie-breakers.
- Successful searches persist preferences to a local properties file and
restore them on startup when possible.
- CLI and GUI surfaces now expose the same effective preference set.
- Product, API, architecture, and build docs were updated to match the
implementation.

## Verification

- `./gradlew test`
- `./gradlew clean check`
- Isolated CLI smoke test for flag-driven search with
`-Duser.home=<temp>`
- Isolated CLI smoke test for persisted interactive defaults with
`-Duser.home=<temp>`

Closes #18
Closes #20
Closes #21
Closes #23

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Surface listing constraint values in results (#50)
commitResults.messageBody:
## Summary

This PR makes the GUI results panel reflect the listing values that
users actually filter on, so the right side of the app better explains
why a listing fits the chosen constraints.

It also includes the small documentation cleanup for duplicated
requirement IDs in `docs/product/scope-mvp.md`.

## What Changed

- add `Walk` and `Transfers` columns to the results table
- keep aircon visible in the table and remove the visible `Match` column
so the default window fits the important columns
- keep the match score in the selected-listing details panel
- split the selected-listing commute area into separate fields for total
commute, transit, walk, transfers, and fare
- update product/design docs so the GUI output description matches the
current UI
- fix duplicated requirement IDs in `docs/product/scope-mvp.md`

## Why

The GUI already used commute, walking, transfer, and aircon constraints
when filtering listings, but some of those values were not clearly
visible in the results table or were compressed in the details panel.

That made it harder for users to understand how a shortlisted listing
related to the boundaries they had set on the left side of the screen.

## Impact

- users can scan shortlist rows for walking time and transfer count
directly
- selected listing details are easier to read because commute facts are
split into separate fields
- the default window layout shows the relevant result columns more
cleanly

## Validation

- manual GUI verification completed:
  - transfer cap reflected in the results table
  - walking cap reflected in the results table
- selected listing details show total, transit, walk, transfers, and
fare separately
  - aircon display remains clear in both table and details
- default window layout rechecked after removing the `Match` table
column and tightening widths
- `./gradlew clean check`

commitResults.messageTitle: Error handling, Javadoc enforcement, and GUI application-layer facade as per professor feedback (#49)
commitResults.messageBody:
## Summary

Pulls together three related changes: clearer domain errors and handling
at CLI/GUI edges, documented public APIs enforced by Checkstyle, and a
thin application facade so JavaFX does not depend directly on
`SearchLogic` orchestration.

## Domain exceptions and error handling

- Adds `MapsAroundYouException` as the base for user-facing failures,
with abstract `DataLoadException` and concrete `DatasetIOException` /
`DatasetIntegrityException` so I/O, schema, and cross-dataset problems
are distinguishable.
- Updates storage and service code to throw the new types where
appropriate.
- Narrows CLI and GUI catch blocks: expected domain errors surface as
before; unexpected failures are logged with context instead of being
handled only as broad `RuntimeException`.
- Chains causes when integer parsing fails (`InvalidInputException`
wrapping `NumberFormatException`).
- Defensively copies result lists in `SearchResponse` for safer handoff
across layers.

## Javadoc and Checkstyle

- Adds Javadoc across public types and methods (CLI, GUI, logic, model,
service, storage, common exceptions).
- Enables Checkstyle `MissingJavadocType` and `MissingJavadocMethod` for
`public` API (`main` ignored) so new public surface stays documented.

## Application layer for the GUI

- Introduces `GuiSearchService` and `DefaultGuiSearchService` in
`mapsaroundyou.app`, wired from `ApplicationFactory`, so the UI depends
on a narrow application API instead of owning `SearchLogic` usage.
- Moves `SearchRequest` and `SearchResponse` into `app` with the facade.
- Adds `package-info.java` describing intended dependency direction
(`gui` → `app` → `logic` / storage).

## How to verify

```bash
./gradlew clean check
```

Run any GUI smoke checks you normally use after that.
## Related Issues

Closes #28
Closes #43
Closes #45
Refs #46

`mapsaroundyou.gui.Launcher`, but the User Guide documented it as the
CLI entry flow.

and document CLI invocation using `runCli` (with args passed via

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge branch 'main' into docs/pr53-max-transfers-guide-fix
Clarify max-transfers no-limit behavior in User Guide (#56)
commitResults.messageBody:
## What changed

This follow-up updates `docs/UserGuide.md` so it matches the GUI
behavior merged in PR #53 for **Max transfers**.

Specifically, the guide now consistently explains that:
- in the GUI, leaving **Max transfers** blank means **No limit**
- the previous warning about blank numeric fields does not apply to that
field
- CLI guidance remains separate: use a high value or keep the default if
you do not want transfer-count filtering

## Why

PR #53 changed the GUI parser and prompt text to support blank `Max
transfers` as unlimited, but the User Guide still described blank
numeric fields as invalid and still nudged users toward setting a high
value instead. That created a user-facing docs mismatch.

## Impact

First-time GUI users should no longer get conflicting instructions when
they try to understand or use the new no-limit behavior.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- Reviewed the merged PR #53 behavior against:
  - `src/main/java/mapsaroundyou/gui/GuiSearchRequestParser.java`
  - `src/main/java/mapsaroundyou/gui/MapsAroundYouGuiApp.java`
- `bash scripts/check-doc-links.sh` could not be run in this environment
because WSL/bash is not installed
- `./gradlew clean check` not run because this is a docs-only follow-up
with no code changes

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge branch 'main' into docs/30-uml-developer-guide
docs: Developer Guide + PlantUML diagrams (#29, #30, #46) (#55)
commitResults.messageBody:
## Summary

Docs-only PR that:

- Adds a canonical **Developer Guide** at
[`docs/developer-guide.md`](docs/developer-guide.md) following the AB3 /
tick-tock structure (Prerequisites -> Setup/Build/Test -> Architecture
-> **Architecture & Coupling** -> Class Diagram -> Implementation ->
Testing -> Contribution workflow -> Extension guidance -> Pitfalls ->
Appendix), linked from the top-level README.
- Introduces five **PlantUML** diagrams under
[`docs/assets/images/`](docs/assets/images/) with rendered SVGs
committed side-by-side: architecture component, full class diagram,
activity (Generate Shortlist), and two sequence diagrams (Generate
Shortlist, Commute Breakdown).
- Replaces the legacy Mermaid Workflow B / Workflow C flowcharts in
[`docs/design/sdd.md`](docs/design/sdd.md) and the ASCII component block
in [`docs/design/architecture.md`](docs/design/architecture.md) with the
new SVG references.
- Adds local render tooling
([`scripts/render-diagrams.sh`](scripts/render-diagrams.sh) /
[`.bat`](scripts/render-diagrams.bat)) that auto-downloads a pinned
PlantUML jar; no Gradle, CI, or Java source changes.

## Issues closed

- Closes #29 - standalone Developer Guide at a stable path covering
architecture, setup/build/test, contribution workflow, and extension
guidance; linked from README.
- Closes #30 - one polished course-ready activity diagram for Generate
Shortlist in the SDD, with a complementary sequence diagram for Commute
Breakdown; note added in architecture.md linking to the DG's coupling
section.
- Closes #46 - class diagram surfaces interface-vs-concrete distinction,
`ApplicationFactory` as composition root, and the
`PersistentSearchLogic` decorator. DG's **Architecture & Coupling**
section documents dependency direction, composition-root rules, and
contributor guidelines for keeping coupling low.

## Notation compliance

All diagrams were passed through an independent Codex notation review
against the CS2103DE / SE-EDU rubric and fixed:

- Class diagram - dashed realization (`..\|>`), association with
multiplicities on both ends, no attribute/association duplication for
enums or records.
- Sequence diagrams - `opt` used where there's no alternative branch
(not `alt`); solid calls + dashed returns; balanced activation bars.
- Activity diagram - `start`/`stop`, square-bracket guards, diamond
decision nodes, no swimlanes.
- Component diagram - solid for uses, dashed for dependency; no
multiplicities at component level.

## Test plan

- [ ] `bash scripts/render-diagrams.sh` produces 5 SVGs with exit code 0
- [ ] GitHub Markdown preview of [SDD](docs/design/sdd.md) shows the new
activity + sequence SVGs inline
- [ ] GitHub Markdown preview of
[architecture.md](docs/design/architecture.md) shows the component SVG
inline
- [ ] GitHub Markdown preview of the new [Developer
Guide](docs/developer-guide.md) shows all 5 SVGs and all internal links
resolve
- [ ] `./gradlew build` still passes with no Java changes
- [ ] Visual review: open each SVG in a browser - readable at zoom, no
Graphviz warnings

commitResults.messageTitle: Feat: Added GUI V1
commitResults.messageBody:
- Introduce mapsaroundyou.gui (MapsAroundYouGuiApp, GuiSearchService, SearchRequest/Response, SearchRow)
- Extract ApplicationFactory for SearchLogic composition; CLI uses it unchanged

commitResults.messageTitle: Merge origin/GUI to apply CI fixes
Fix CI: satisfy Checkstyle line length and SpotBugs on SearchResponse
commitResults.messageBody:
Wrap the score column cell value factory in MapsAroundYouGuiApp so the
line stays within the 120-character limit.

Add a compact constructor on SearchResponse that assigns results via
List.copyOf to avoid exposing internal mutable list state (EI_EXPOSE_REP /
EI_EXPOSE_REP2)

commitResults.messageTitle: GUI: Apply dark theme to Settings window
commitResults.messageBody:
The Settings modal uses its own Scene, which never received the dark
theme stylesheet, so it stayed light while the main window followed
the preference.
Let's extend applyDarkMode to accept additional scenes and pass the
settings Scene when opening and when toggling so it matches the main
window.

commitResults.messageTitle: UiSettingsStore: Harden prefs access against runtime failures
commitResults.messageBody:
Preferences I/O can throw unchecked exceptions when access is
denied or the backing store is unusable, which would surface in
the GUI during load or toggle.
Let's treat persistence as best-effort for those failures: use
defaults on read and skip writes when the API throws.

commitResults.messageTitle: MapsAroundYouGuiApp.java: Resolve import merge conflict
commitResults.messageBody: Imports from the UX branch and main both touched JavaFX controls. The merge duplicated Tooltip while main added Image for the stage icon. Keep a single Tooltip import alongside Image.

commitResults.messageTitle: Error handling: Introduce domain exception hierarchy
commitResults.messageBody:
Bundled data failures and user input errors share few types, and CLI/GUI edges catch RuntimeException broadly, which hides causes and blends I/O with schema or cross-dataset issues.

Let's,
* introduce MapsAroundYouException with DatasetIOException,
  DatasetIntegrityException, and an abstract DataLoadException parent
* narrow CLI/GUI catches, log unexpected failures, and preserve causes
  when wrapping parse failures
* defensively copy SearchResponse result lists for safer exposure

This clarifies failure modes at module boundaries and improves diagnosability without changing happy-path behaviuor.

commitResults.messageTitle: docs: Add Javadoc and enforce it in Checkstyle
commitResults.messageBody:
Public APIs were hard to skim in the IDE and onboarding docs because
Javadoc was sparse and inconsistent across packages.

Let's,
* document public types and methods with purpose, parameters, returns, and throws where callers care
* add concise notes for CSV helpers, CLI/GUI control flow, and model record components
* enable MissingJavadocType/MissingJavadocMethod for public API
This keeps contributor-facing documentation aligned with the exception types and repository contracts.

commitResults.messageTitle: app: Introduce GuiSearchService interface for JavaFX
commitResults.messageBody:
The GUI constructed SearchLogic and a concrete helper in the gui package, and search DTOs lived next to JavaFX types. That ties the UI to orchestration details and blurs the application boundary.

Let's add a GuiSearchService interface and DefaultGuiSearchService in app, move SearchRequest and SearchResponse to app, and wire the facade
from ApplicationFactory with package documentation for dependency flow.

The UI now depends on a narrow application type; persistence stays behind SearchLogic and repository interfaces.

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: Update README and script to reflect changes in CSV file names and note handling
feat: enhance search logic with result limit validation and update data generation guide
